summary: Insurance broker Hub International Holdings Inc. is seeking to raise about $3 billion in its initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Indians Buy Record Alternative Fuel Cars Even as Ethanol Protests Rise
summary: Indians are swayed more by economics than ideology.

title: Tech Disruptors: SailPoint’s Mills on Governance for AI Agents
summary: The governance landscape for AI agents is evolving amid new developments around agentic workflows, says SailPoint President Matt Mills. He joins Bloomberg Intelligence analyst Mandeep Singh on this episode of the Tech Disruptors podcast. The conversation also explores how agentic AI could reshape identity governance, from internal functions and product capabilities to potential shifts in pricing models and customer adoption.

title: Gold Falls as Traders Assess Fed Rate Outlook Ahead of Minutes
summary: Gold dropped as traders weighed the outlook for the Federal Reserve’s interest rate path at the start of a week in which the minutes of the US central bank’s last meeting are expected to shed light on policymakers’ thinking.

title: I Don't Expect The Fed To Hike In July Says Lavorgna
summary: President Donald Trump rang the opening bell for the New York Stock Exchange and the Nasdaq from the Oval Office to mark the launch of Trump Accounts, a new investment vehicle for children. Joe Lavorgna, Chief Economist of SMBC Americas joins to discuss.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Lone Pine Gains 43% in First Half on Both Long and Short Bets
summary: Lone Pine Capital posted a 43% gain for its hedge fund investors through June, notching one of its best-ever starts to a year and landing it among 2026’s top performers.

title: Distressed Funds Explore a Swap of Strategy Holdings After Rout
summary: Distressed-debt funds that snapped up Strategy Inc.’s beaten-down preferred shares are in talks with one of the company’s bankers about swapping them for other securities, pitching it as a win-win as the relentless rout in crypto puts the Bitcoin accumulator in an increasingly tough spot.

title: Tech Giants Drive Stock Gains in AI Trade Revival: Markets Wrap
summary: A rally in several technology powerhouses lifted stocks amid speculation that the artificial-intelligence trade that has powered the bull market has more room to run.

title: New Zealand Set to Raise Key Rate, Decision Less of a Slam Dunk
summary: New Zealand’s central bank is poised to raise its key interest rate this week, most economists and traders reckon, beginning a gradual unwinding of stimulus as it aims to head off emerging inflationary pressures.

title: Trump Is Not a Man Who Plays By The Rules, Says Daalder
summary: Ivo Daalder, former US Ambassador to NATO under President Barack Obama, joined Balance of Power to discuss the the upcoming NATO meeting in Turkey. Daalder said the best outcome from the NATO summit this week is "that the alliance lives another day." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Advertising Technology Firm Criteo Attracts Vista Equity-Backed Takeover Offer
summary: Vista Equity Partners and the investment firm Quinti Capital have teamed up on a takeover offer for French advertising-technology company Criteo SA, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: SEC Mulls New ETF Rules as $16T Boom Disrupts Status Quo
summary: Bloomberg's Isabelle Lee joins Scarlet Fu, Katie Greifeld, and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." The SEC is signaling a potential rethink of how it oversees ETFs after a recent wave of filings for prediction-market ETFs prompted fresh scrutiny of the existing regulatory framework.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ontario, Alberta Pitch Cross-Canada Oil Pipeline to Ease US Reliance
summary: Alberta and Ontario proposed a crude oil pipeline and energy corridor across Canada as a way of reducing the country’s dependence on the US.

title: Public Spending in Mexico Ends Prolonged Fall in Fixed Investment
summary: A key measure of investments in Mexico snapped back in April driven largely by government spending while private investment lagged, offering scant relief to President Claudia Sheinbaum’s push for stronger economic growth.

title: Trump Bitcoin Reserve Faces Hurdles as Departments Seek Control
summary: The Trump administration’s plan to create a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve has been complicated by two government departments vying to run it, alongside questions about which has the legal authority to do so.

title: Retail Investors Use ETFs as a Tool For Alpha: Zhang
summary: Sylvia Zhang, executive director and assistant head of global index at E Fund Management, joins Scarlet Fu, Katie Greifeld, and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." They discusses China's investor trends and its booming ETF industry. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Difficult Era For Markets Comping Up: Howe
summary: Neil Howe, portfolio manager at Hedgeye Asset Management and co-author of The Fourth Turning, joins Scarlet Fu, Katie Greifeld, and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." They discuss the Hedgeye Fourth Turning ETF (ticker: HEFT). Howe predicts the next ten years in markets are going to see a fundamental change compared to the last forty. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Nuclear-Arms Exposure in ESG Funds Rises 95%
summary:
Bloomberg Intelligence's Shaheen Contractor joins Scarlet Fu, Katie Greifeld, and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ."

ESG and sustainable funds' saw 95% increase in exposure to nuclear stocks since June 2025. The increase in exposure was likely aided by EU guidance last year that left nuclear arms out of its controversial-weapons definition. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Expanding US Economy to Kids "Revolutionary": Paglia
summary: Anna Paglia, executive vice president and chief business officer, State Street Investment Management, joins Scarlet Fu, Katie Greifeld, and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." Money flowing into President Donald Trump’s newly created accounts for children will initially be invested in a State Street ETF, as the US Treasury Department prepares to roll out the program.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Tech Stocks Extend Rally as Dip Buyers Pounce on Chip Pullback
summary: US stocks climbed on Monday as investors jumped on technology shares after the blistering artificial-intelligence trade wavered heading into the holiday weekend.

title: AI Trade Faces Big Test as Trump Rings Opening Bell | Open Interest 7/6/2026
summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." It's a  pivotal week for the AI trade: SpaceX joins the Nasdaq 100, Samsung reports earnings, and SK Hynix eyes a US debut. Plus, President Trump rings the opening bell for Trump Accounts, Xbox cuts 3,200 jobs, and we talk about Wall Street’s next big sports play. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ondas to Buy Military Drone Maker DZYNE for $875.8 Million
summary: Ondas Inc. agreed to buy autonomous aircraft maker DZYNE Technologies in an $875.8 million cash-and stock-deal that positions Ondas as a full-service autonomous defense technology platform.

title: Memory Chipmaker SK Hynix Kicks Off $28 Billion US Listing
summary:
SK Hynix kicked off the formal marketing process for its US listing on Monday, as the South Korean chipmaker looks to capitalize on surging investor demand for the high-flying memory-chip sector.
Bailey Lipschultz, Bloomberg News Senior Equities Reporter, joined Bloomberg's Paul Sweeney to highlight the formal marketing process for SK Hynix and its US listing. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Activists Take on More Campaigns While Avoiding Proxy Fights
summary: Campaigns by activist investors have hit an all-time high this year even as the portion of them leading to proxy battles continues to fall.

title: Ardian’s Santé Cie Seeks to Enter French Restructuring Procedure
summary: Santé Cie SAS is seeking to enter French restructuring proceedings after some of its cash assets were seized as part of an investigation from the authorities into alleged corruption and money laundering.

title: Sports-Related Deals on the Rise, Says William Blair’s IB Head
summary: Acquisitions in the sports industry are set to increase as investors leverage a slew of different approaches to the fast-growing sector, according to William Blair & Co.’s head of investment banking.

title: Dental Chain Owner Aspen Drops WellNow Sale as Lenders Lawyer Up
summary: The Aspen Group has indicated it’s no longer moving ahead with a planned sale of its urgent care clinic chain WellNow, after seeking buyers amid an effort to address a looming debt deadline that’s also prompted its lenders to retain lawyers.

title: Peace Deal Doesn’t Restore Pre-War Situation, ECB’s Schnabel Says
summary: Peace efforts in the Middle East have helped bring energy prices down rapidly but haven’t restored the position the world was in before hostilities erupted, according to European Central Bank Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel.

title: The Restaurant Economy Doesn’t Look K-Shaped
summary: It looks like a broad based boom.

title: Boston Airport Borrows $812 Million for Revamp as Traffic Soars
summary: The busiest airport in New England is tapping the municipal bond market to remodel its facilities and keep up with passenger growth.

title: Hungary Sells €3 Billion in Post-Orban Eurobond Return
summary: Hungary sold €3 billion ($3.4 billion) at its first international bond issuance since Viktor Orban’s ouster in April’s election triggered a plunge in borrowing costs.

title: Belgium Fumes After FIFA Clears Balogun Following Trump Call
summary: FIFA's decision to overturn US striker Folarin Balogun's World Cup suspension — after an intervention from Donald Trump — has caused fury in Belgium. Max Ramsay explains why the reversal is overshadowing their crucial match against Team USA. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trans Mountain 3.0 Revives Indigenous Pipeline Ownership Push
summary: Canada’s announcement last week that Trans Mountain Corp. will build a 1 million-barrel-a-day oil pipeline along the government-owned company’s existing Alberta-to-British Columbia route is rekindling Indigenous interest in acquiring a stake in the company.
